task_completion.description: Verify that a new document named 'Non-Functional Requirements' with the prefix 'NFR' has been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a document tab, title, or tree view item named 'Non-Functional Requirements'. Requirement IDs within this document should start with 'NFR'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Security' section is present in the new 'Non-Functional Requirements' document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a heading or section titled 'Security' within the 'Non-Functional Requirements' document.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Security' section is placed at the top level of the new document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Check the indentation or numbering hierarchy in the 'Non-Functional Requirements' document. The 'Security' section should not be nested under any other section.

task_completion.description: Verify that the child requirements of the 'Security' section were moved along with it.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for requirements nested under the 'Security' section in the new document containing the text 'AES-256', 'TLS 1.3', and 'Argon2'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Security' section is no longer present in the original SRS document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the SRS document view and ensure the 'Security' section is absent.

task_completion.description: Verify that the project has been saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the absence of unsaved changes indicators (such as asterisks next to document names) or a 'Saved' status message in the application.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the requirements management application's GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the application's menus, buttons, and drag-and-drop or cut-paste features, rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not edit the underlying project files directly in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no screenshots of text editors (like VS Code, nano, or vim) modifying JSON files directly.

integrity.description: Verify that the moved section and requirements maintain proper application formatting.
integrity.visual_evidence: The moved items should appear as native application elements (sections and requirements with IDs) rather than plain text pasted into a single field.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target new document must be named 'Non-Functional Requirements' with the prefix 'NFR'. The section to be moved is titled 'Security'. The child requirements of the 'Security' section contain the signatures 'AES-256', 'TLS 1.3', and 'Argon2'. The original document is 'SRS'.
